calendar.matters:
(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via Zoom, for Jose Chavez and Grace Vasquez, Petitioners.

Ms. Ebey reports that the matter has settled and requests that the court take the matter off calendar.

The court grants the request, and this matter is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar Feb-08-2022 Continued To May-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Grace Vasquez and Jose Chavez (deceased).
Greer Smith appeared by zoom for Francis Gonzales.

The court questions whether this case is resolved. Ms. Ebey stated that it is settled, that she submitted fifteen drafts of the settlement agreement, that she finally got a signed settlement agreement and asked the court to continue the matter to work out the small details and the issue of the damaged personal property. Ms. Greer stated that they have a settlement agreement, that her client has moved out of the property, that they have a realtor, and the property is being put on the market, and that she doesn't see why the court should micromanage this. Both counsels confirmed that the documents were executed by their clients, that the terms were explained to the parties, and that the documents were signed by both counsel and approved as to form. Discussion was held as to whether it makes sense to continue the petition or take if off calendar, the issue of Ms. Ebey withdrawing the petition once the property is sold, the expense of keeping the matter on calendar, the length of the continuance, etc. Ms. Ebey stated that there isn't going to be a petition for approval of the settlement agreement as it is not the agreement. Ms. Greer expressed that she would like a petition for approval filed. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales as Successor Co-Trustee, etc. is continued to May 10, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on sale of the property and to see whether or not the petition is going to be withdrawn.
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Continued To Jan-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar Oct-26-2021 Continued To Dec-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Grace Vasquez, Petitioner; Greer Smith, via ZOOM, for Vicky Gonzales, Respondent; Maurice Gonzales, via ZOOM, Ms. Gonzales' son and Power of Attorney.

The court confirms with the parties that they have met with the court's Settlement Officer Pang Ly. Ms. Smith reports that the parties continue working with Ms. Ly and that she has regularly provided Ms. Ly with updates regarding ongoing settlement discussions. Ms. Ebey reports that the parties are close to signing a settlement agreement and that a real estate agent has been approved to list the property for sale. Ms. Smith reports that one of the conditions of the agreement is for her client to receive funds to move out but that her client is unable to move out until the settlement agreement has been signed and the funds distributed. The court confirms with the counsel that the last contact with Ms. Ly was last week and encourages the parties to continue to work with her to facilitate finalizing the agreement sooner.

After additional discussion, the Petition to Suspend and Remove, etc., is continued to Dec-14-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Continued To Oct-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

(1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
Off Calendar Jul-28-2021 Continued To Sep-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Grace Vasquez, Petitioner; Greer Smith, via ZOOM, for Objector Vicky Gonzalez, via ZOOM; Maurice Gonzalez, via ZOOM.

Ms. Smith begins by reporting that the parties continue to negotiate. Ms. Ebey confirms however, reports that discussions have been ongoing for about a year and asks the court for a ruling. Ms. Smith believes that this matter can be settled but that her client needs a bit more time. The court agrees that settlement discussions might resolve this matter and avoid unnecessary expenses. Accordingly, the court will be ordering the parties to coordinate with court settlement Officer Pang Ly to schedule a settlement conference. The court will draft the order and notify the parties via U.S. mail.

The Petition to Suspend and Remove Frances Vicky Gonzalez is continued to September 22, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.
